title: The Time Traveler's Wife
author: Audrey Niffenegger
review: 1.5 Amazing concept. Terribly executed. Such a waste. Mountains of filler. Cringey ethnic stereotypes. One dimensional pretentious upper middle class characters who completely miss the mark of being "cool". Pseud references to Greek literature and Marxism. Creepy groomer Henry. Poor boring Clare.

title: Children of Ruin (Children of Time, #2)
author: Adrian Tchaikovsky
review:
Some interesting, but sparse ideas that have been repetitively dotted throughout, and then buffered by a pretentious slog of adderall fueled waffle - ultimately giving the impression of a word count needlessly inflated fivefold. Some nice space horror though.


Fuck Fabian.

title: Recursion
author: Blake Crouch
review:
1.7
Yeah not great. Really strong start - fun plot and the book looked to be heading towards new territory. However, as the book progressed, it really broke the immersion when entry level philosophy and pop-sci ideas were being seriously debated by the characters, characters that became consistently one dimensional and unable to present any well founded insights about concepts that they had supposedly been investigating for lifetimes. Especially in the second half of the novel, the writing felt like a collection of fake deep ramblings scraped from cliche Facebook quotes pages.

Riddled with plot holes.

Frustrating misuse of scientific terminology, and a shadowy approximation of what scientific academia and research is.
Trying to calculate “the Schwarzschild radius of a memories” lmao

The characters weren’t particularly likeable or memorable.

Not for me.

A btech version of The First Fifteen Lives of Harry August, or the Time Traveller's Wife
